violations.text: Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 12/08/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the walk-in cooler including the fan guards and display shelves have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to prevent the accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 12/08/2025

The ceiling in the deli and food preparation areas is covered with tiles that are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. Establishment is to install ceiling tiles that meet minimum requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 06/08/2026

Protective shielding is not provided for the lights in the food preparation area. Light bulbs must be shielded, coated, or otherwise shatter-resistant in areas where there is exposed food; clean equipment, utensils, and linens; or unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2026

Deli meat was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. All deli meat was gone through, and proper dates were placed on food items. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/21/2025

Various food items were observed held between 42-45 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Potato salad was observed at 45 DF and was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. All other food items were evaluated and moved into the walk-in cooler. Establishment is not to store any TCS foods in the ready-to-eat cooler until it is able to hold an internal food temperature of 41 DF or less. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025

Deli meat and cheese was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Dates were added to food products at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025

The Ready-to-eat cooler was observed holding an ambient temperature of 45 DF. Cold holding units must hold an ambient temperature of at least 41 DF. The door to the cooler was observed with a gap and in poor repair. Equipment must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/14/2025
